skill.name: lesson-builder
skill.description: 帮助用户通过讨论完成课程大纲和课件。当用户说"备课"、"做课件"、"准备课程"时触发。
skill.summary: A collaborative lesson planning assistant that creates course outlines and teaching materials through interactive discussion.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is purely documentation markdown with no executable code. Backtick characters are used for markdown code block formatting in workflow diagrams, not shell execution. No cryptographic operations exist - the 'weak crypto' flag incorrectly identified text content. This is a safe documentation-only skill describing a lesson planning assistant.

content.user_title: Build Complete Lesson Plans Through Discussion
content.value_statement: Teachers struggle to transform their ideas into structured course materials. This skill guides you through a collaborative discussion to create ready-to-use lesson outlines and teaching materials.
actual_capabilities: Conduct interactive discussions to understand your teaching goals and audience
Create structured course outlines with module breakdowns and time allocations
Generate complete teaching materials including concepts, examples, and demonstrations
Support two collaboration modes: fast iterative or step-by-step confirmation
Produce both instructor guides and student-facing materials
Offer flexible output formats including Feishu cloud document format
limitations: Only produces text-based documents - cannot create actual slides or multimedia content
Requires active user participation and input throughout the discussion process
Focuses on single lesson creation rather than full curriculum design
Output is in Chinese and English - may not support other languages

use_cases.title: Prepare a Technical Training Session
use_cases.description: You need to teach developers about a new framework. The skill helps you structure the content, allocate time for each concept, and create detailed teaching materials with code examples.

use_cases.title: Create Online Course Content
use_cases.description: You have expertise to share but need help organizing it into a coherent lesson. The skill guides you through defining learning objectives and building a complete course outline.

use_cases.title: Iterate Existing Course Materials
use_cases.description: You have a rough lesson plan that needs refinement. The skill helps you reorganize content, add missing elements, and produce polished teaching materials.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Lesson Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to prepare a 2-hour lesson on [topic] for [audience]. I want to cover [key points]. Help me create a course outline using the fast creation mode.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - quick start with basic inputs

prompt_templates.title: Full Course Development
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm teaching [subject] to [learners]. They need to learn [skills]. I have [existing materials/timeframe]. Guide me through creating both the outline and complete teaching materials step by step.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Existing Curriculum
prompt_templates.prompt: Here's my current lesson plan: [paste outline]. Review it and help me improve the structure, add missing components, and create detailed teaching materials for each module.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Collaboration Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to create a lesson on [topic]. I prefer [collaboration style]. My constraints are [time/resources]. Ask me questions to understand my needs and help me build a complete course package with outline, materials, and supplementary guides.

output_examples.input: I need to teach Python basics to high school students in 90 minutes
output_examples.output: A structured outline with 4 modules: Introduction to Programming (15 min), Variables and Data Types (25 min), Control Flow (30 min), Hands-on Project (20 min). Includes time buffers, required materials list, and student prerequisites.

output_examples.input: Create teaching materials for a module on API design
output_examples.output: Complete module content including: core concepts (REST principles, HTTP methods), real-world case study (analyzing a popular API), step-by-step demonstration (building an endpoint), and key takeaways summary with practice exercises.

best_practices: Start with the fast creation mode to get a draft quickly, then iterate with specific feedback
Be specific about your audience and their background when describing your teaching needs
Review the framework outline before proceeding to detailed content creation to avoid major revisions later
anti_patterns: Providing vague topic descriptions without clarifying the target audience or learning objectives
Skipping the outline confirmation step and requesting full materials immediately
Expecting the skill to generate multimedia content like slides or videos - it produces text documents only

faq.question: What is the difference between lesson-builder and course-designer?
faq.answer: lesson-builder focuses on creating individual lessons with outlines and teaching materials. course-designer is for comprehensive curriculum planning with detailed learner personas and full course structure.

faq.question: Do I need to have my content prepared before starting?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed to help you develop ideas from scratch. You only need a general topic and understanding of your audience. The discussion process will help you clarify and organize your thoughts.

faq.question: Can this skill create actual PowerPoint slides?
faq.answer: No. This skill produces text-based documents including outlines and teaching materials in markdown or cloud document format. You would need to convert the content to slides using other tools.

faq.question: How long does it take to create a complete lesson?
faq.answer: The time depends on your collaboration mode and lesson complexity. Fast mode can produce a draft in one session. The interactive discussion approach typically takes 15-30 minutes for a basic lesson outline.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-technical subjects?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works for any teaching topic - technical training, soft skills, academic subjects, or professional development. The structure adapts to your content area.

faq.question: What format are the output documents?
faq.answer: The skill produces markdown-formatted text documents. It mentions Feishu (Lark) cloud document format as an option. You can copy the output to any document editor or convert to other formats as needed.
